page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARM64 architecture, specifically the AWS Neoverse-V2 (ARMv9) model running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles a massive 120,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 300 Gbps baseline bandwidth. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier status in both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations. Database and web serving capabilities are highly optimized, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations, Passmark database operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls in the average tier.

The server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for massive storage and memory-intensive workloads, leveraging the power-efficient ARM64 architecture to deliver high throughput per resource. However, a key tradeoff is the lack of hardware accelerators like GPUs and x86-specific instruction sets, which limits its suitability for heavy GPU-accelerated AI training or legacy x86 applications. Additionally, text generation for LLM inference is weak. This server is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, software compilation, and intensive multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by 192 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and an extensive 120,000 GB local NVMe SSD storage array, backed by a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database throughput, particularly in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. Single-core performance and LLM text generation remain average to poor. Lacking dedicated GPUs or x86 instruction sets, this server trades specialized AI acceleration for massive storage capacity and high memory density. It is highly cost-efficient for scale-out workloads, making it an optimal fit for large-scale database systems, high-throughput web serving, and intensive multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 ARM64 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, 120 TB NVMe SSD storage, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is an ARM64 bare-metal server with 192 cores, 1.5 TB DDR5 RAM, and 120 TB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server combining massive local NVMe storage, high-bandwidth DDR5 memory, and 192 dedicated ARM64 cores for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
120,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier performance in multi-core processing, Redis operations, and database tasks
Best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data processing
